title: pow: AppState::Inner PoW fields
description:
Add PoW state to AppState::Inner in relay/src/state.rs.

What: Add three new fields for PoW server state; no AppState::new signature change.

Algorithm:
1. Add to relay/Cargo.toml: no new deps needed (rand already present via nie-core)
   Add to relay/src/state.rs imports: use rand::RngCore; use rand::rngs::OsRng;
2. Add fields to Inner struct:
   pub pow_difficulty: std::sync::atomic::AtomicU8,
   pub pow_server_salt: [u8; 32],
   pub pow_replay_set: dashmap::DashMap<[u8; 16], std::time::Instant>,
3. In AppState::new(): initialize the new fields:
   - pow_server_salt: generate with OsRng::fill_bytes(&mut buf)
   - pow_difficulty: AtomicU8::new(0)  — 0 = disabled
   - pow_replay_set: DashMap::new()
4. Derive salt: let mut salt = [0u8; 32]; OsRng.fill_bytes(&mut salt);
5. In AppState::disconnect(): add self.inner.pow_replay_set.remove(&pub_id.0) — NO, replay set keys are h16 not pub_id; skip disconnect cleanup
6. Add helper method:
   pub fn pow_difficulty(&self) -> u8 { self.inner.pow_difficulty.load(Ordering::Relaxed) }
   pub fn set_pow_difficulty(&self, d: u8) { self.inner.pow_difficulty.store(d, Ordering::Relaxed); }
   pub fn pow_server_salt(&self) -> &[u8; 32] { &self.inner.pow_server_salt }

IMPORTANT: Do NOT change AppState::new() signature — avoids updating 10+ test files

Defensive programming:
- Use OsRng (not thread_rng) for server_salt — requires cryptographic randomness
- pow_server_salt must never appear in any log statement
- AtomicU8 with Relaxed ordering is sufficient — difficulty changes are not synchronized with individual requests
acceptance_criteria: cargo build -p nie-relay passes; state.rs has pow_difficulty, pow_server_salt, pow_replay_set in Inner; AppState::new signature unchanged
close_reason: state.rs: added pow_server_salt, pow_difficulty (AtomicU8), pow_replay_set to Inner; pow_difficulty/set_pow_difficulty/pow_server_salt helpers on AppState; AppState::new unchanged
